questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Write Once Run Anywhere' (WORA) in Java.
keyPoints: Java programs written on one platform can run on any other platform with Java Virtual Machine
No recompilation needed
Achieved through bytecode and JVM
Platform independence is main feature

questions.question: Differentiate between JDK, JRE, and JVM.
keyPoints: JVM - executes bytecode, provides runtime environment
JRE - includes JVM + libraries needed to run Java programs
JDK - includes JRE + development tools (compiler, debugger)
JDK is for developers, JRE for end users

questions.question: Explain the different primitive data types in Java with their sizes.
keyPoints: Integer types: byte(8-bit), short(16-bit), int(32-bit), long(64-bit)
Floating point: float(32-bit), double(64-bit)
Character: char(16-bit unsigned)
Boolean: boolean (represents true/false)

questions.question: What are control statements in Java? Explain selection statements.
keyPoints: Control statements manage program flow
Selection: if-else, switch-case
if-else for conditional execution
switch for multiple branching
break and continue used for loop control

questions.question: Explain the difference between while and do-while loops with examples.
keyPoints: while loop: checks condition first, may not execute if condition is false
do-while: executes body at least once before checking condition
while: pre-test loop
do-while: post-test loop

questions.question: What is a constructor in Java? Explain its types.
keyPoints: Special method to initialize objects
Same name as class, no return type
Types: Default (no-arg), Parameterized
Called automatically when object is created
Can be overloaded

questions.question: Explain the four pillars of Object Oriented Programming.
keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods, data hiding
Inheritance: Acquiring properties from parent class
Polymorphism: Taking many forms (overloading/overriding)
Abstraction: Hiding complex implementation details

questions.question: Explain the Java program execution process from source code to execution.
keyPoints: Write source code in .java file
Compile using javac to generate .class bytecode
Bytecode is platform-independent
Class loader loads bytecode into JVM
JVM verifies bytecode (Bytecode Verifier)
Interpreter/ JIT compiler executes bytecode
Converts to machine-specific code for execution

questions.question: Explain the different access modifiers in Java with their scope.
keyPoints: private: accessible only within same class
default (package-private): within same package
protected: same package + subclasses in different packages
public: accessible from anywhere
Show table comparing all modifiers for class, method, variable access

questions.question: Explain the concept of classes and objects in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Class: Blueprint/template with attributes and methods
Object: Instance of class occupying memory
Creating class: class ClassName { fields; methods; }
Creating object: new ClassName()
Demonstrate with example like Student class with name, rollno, marks

questions.question: What is type casting in Java? Explain implicit and explicit type casting with examples.
keyPoints: Type casting: converting one data type to another
Implicit (widening): automatic conversion from smaller to larger type
Explicit (narrowing): manual conversion using cast operator
Example implicit: int to double
Example explicit: double to int (data loss possible)
